decisions: Retained all daily logs (04-21 through 04-23): oldest is 3 days old, all within 7-day window — no deletion candidates

Skipped tier promotion after reviewing 4 daily log files (04-21: 1 bootstrap entry, 04-22: 2 maintenance entries, 04-23: 3 maintenance entries, TODAY: 3 maintenance entries) — all entries are maintenance heartbeats with no user-generated content to promote
observations: Brain has been idle for 4 consecutive days since bootstrap — daily logs contain only maintenance consolidation entries
All four memory tiers (core, semantic, episodic, procedural) remain empty of user-generated content
Daily log coverage is perfect (4/4 days with sessions have logs) but all content is maintenance-only
recommendations: If brain remains idle after 7 days, consider consolidating the maintenance-only daily logs into a single note rather than retaining them individually
First real user session should populate core/PREFERENCES.md and core/LEARNINGS.md to establish baseline
processImprovements: [self-critique] The consolidation process runs faithfully but produces no value when the brain is idle — 4 days of consolidation reports that all say 'nothing to do'. The maintenance overhead (commits, reports) may be disproportionate to the benefit during idle periods.
[proposal] Consider adding an idle-detection heuristic: if the last N consolidation runs found nothing to promote, reduce consolidation frequency or produce a minimal report instead of a full one.

[blocked] The maintenance-guard.sh script is in the base-brain but the MAINTENANCE.md instructions reference it as 'scripts/maintenance-guard.sh' (relative), requiring the agent to locate it in the base-brain directory. Could be clearer.
